name: Wisdom: Multi-tenant jobs tracking platform
description: FastAPI + MongoDB backend, React + TypeScript SPA, optional Tauri desktop shell, Podman/Docker-first ops, and extensive security/deployment documentation.
longDescription: Wisdom is a multi-tenant job-tracking product: a secure FastAPI API on MongoDB (JWT auth, company-scoped data, rich domain flows described in backend documentation) and a Vite-powered React frontend with TypeScript. The stack runs under Podman or Docker Compose variants (development, Gunicorn, Nuitka-hardened builds, registry-based pulls). A Tauri desktop wrapper reuses the web UI with its own security guidance. Documentation covers RBAC, metrics, reporting, webhooks, environment variables, and incident response—operations are treated as a first-class concern.
features: Multi-tenant REST API with documented flows (auth, jobs, clients, processos, companies, branding)
React SPA with shared environment configuration and production build pipeline
Multiple compose topologies for dev, scaled API workers, and compiled backend options
Tauri desktop packaging with security-focused documentation
Windows and shell manage menus for repeatable deployments
Deep documentation index for security architecture and data protection
highlights: Security and operations treated as parallel tracks to feature work
Container-native workflows with GHCR and EasyPanel-oriented notes
Desktop and web clients sharing one API contract
challenges: Multi-tenant SaaS-style systems need consistent RBAC stories, safe secret distribution across compose files, and observable production paths.
solutions: Centralise documentation for env vars, provide hardened deployment profiles (Gunicorn/Nuitka), and document websocket, metrics, and webhook flows explicitly.
security: JWT-based API authentication with documented rotation practices
Dedicated security architecture and incident response documentation
Separation of platform superadmin vs tenant-safe RBAC described in backend feature docs
Guidance for Podman rootless and secret handling in deployment docs
